summary: Conjecture 3.1 from arXiv:1909.07141 remains open as of May 2026. The n=2 case is settled by the Stromquist–Woodall theorem, but the conjecture is already open for n=3. The paper itself establishes f(n) ≤ 3n−4 cuts, while the conjecture would imply the optimal bound f(n) = 2n−2; no subsequent paper resolving or substantially advancing Conjecture 3.1 was found in the indexed literature.
search_queries: arXiv 1909.07141 "disproportionate division" Crew Narayanan Spirkl citing
notes: No follow-up paper resolving Conjecture 3.1 was found across three web searches and two fetches (arXiv abstract page; mass-partitions survey arXiv:2010.00478). The paper was published in Bull. London Math. Soc. 52 (2020) 885–890. Confidence is medium rather than high because the conjecture has been open for ~7 years, making the absence of a resolution mildly surprising but not conclusive.
